comments.text: For the "almost all integers" case of the problem, the mentioned result of [Wo96] has been improved. Kolountzakis (On the additive complements of the primes and sets of similar growth; Acta Arith. 77 (1996), 1–8) showed an upper bound $O(\log(x)\log\log(x))$. A further improvement is in the paper [Ru98c] of Ruzsa mentioned already: Theorem 1 shows that one can achieve $O(\omega(x)\log x)$ for any function $\omega$ that diverges as $x\to\infty$. Additionally, although Ruzsa confirms the third question, he notes that it remains unclear in the "almost all integers" setting, and leaves a few versions of this as conjectures. (The reference was located by GPT-5.) (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
